top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer cites the verse's specific lexical signature explicitly: dhyāyataḥ and krodhaḥ appear in the engineering body, and the structural claim (first half of the cascade: brooding (dhyāna) → attachment (saṅga) → desire (kāma) → anger (krodha)) maps to the verse's two pādas. The named-tools stack (Postgres, Slack) is verse-appropriate. No other verse in the Gita has this specific lexical chain.
